title: Build Repeatable Test Fixture with Predefined Directives
description: Create a self-contained fixture project under tests/fixture/ containing: (1) small Express API + React frontend (10-15 files), (2) 3-4 predefined directives of varying weight, (3) 1-2 pre-selected SWE-bench Lite issues, (4) validation script that checks post-run file state against expected outcomes.

dod.criterion: tests/fixture/ contains 10-15 source files (Express API + React frontend) with intentional issues
3-4 predefined directives exist with directive.json, directive.md, and expected-outcomes.json
validate.sh checks post-run file state against expected outcomes and reports pass/fail

tasks.title: Scaffold fixture directory with Express API + React frontend
tasks.user_scenario: When a developer runs the benchmark suite, they have a realistic but small target codebase that exercises multi-file, multi-directory changes.
tasks.scope: Create tests/fixture/ with a minimal but realistic project: Express API (3-4 route files, 1 middleware, 1 db utility) and React frontend (3-4 components, 1 hook, 1 API client). Total 10-15 source files. Code should be intentionally imperfect — containing a real bug, a missing feature gap, and a refactor opportunity.

dod.criterion: tests/fixture/ directory exists with Express API (server/) and React frontend (src/) subdirectories
Total source file count is between 10 and 15 files (excluding package.json and config)
Code contains at least one intentional bug, one missing feature gap, and one refactor opportunity
A tests/fixture/README.md documents the project structure and lists the intentional issues with file:line references

tasks.title: Create 3-4 predefined directives of varying weight with expected outcomes
tasks.user_scenario: When the benchmark runs, it feeds each predefined directive through the pipeline and compares actual file changes against documented expected outcomes.
tasks.scope: Create 3-4 directives under tests/fixture/.context/directives/, each with directive.json and directive.md. Directives: lightweight bug fix, medium feature addition, heavyweight refactor. Each includes expected-outcomes.json.

dod.criterion: 3-4 directive directories exist under tests/fixture/.context/directives/ each with valid directive.json and directive.md
Each directive.md has a clear objective, context section referencing specific fixture files, and success criteria
Each directive directory contains expected-outcomes.json with files_modified, files_created, files_deleted, and expected_patterns
Directives span at least 2 different weight classes (lightweight, medium, or heavyweight)
Expected outcomes are achievable — each expected file modification targets a file that exists in the fixture

tasks.title: Add 1-2 SWE-bench Lite issues as predefined directives
tasks.user_scenario: The benchmark includes industry-standard SWE-bench issues so pipeline quality can be compared against external baselines.
tasks.scope: Select 1-2 SWE-bench Lite issues that fit the fixture project's scale. Create directives with expected-outcomes.json with the known solution's file changes.

dod.criterion: 1-2 additional directive directories exist under tests/fixture/.context/directives/ with valid directive.json, directive.md, and expected-outcomes.json
Each directive.md cites the original SWE-bench issue ID or describes the adapted issue source
The buggy code targeted by each issue exists in the fixture source files
Expected-outcomes.json for each contains the verified correct fix patterns

tasks.title: Build validation script that checks post-run outcomes against expectations
tasks.user_scenario: After running the pipeline against fixture directives, a developer runs a single validation command that reports pass/fail per directive.
tasks.scope: Create tests/fixture/validate.sh (bash script, no external deps beyond jq and grep) that takes a directive ID, reads expected-outcomes.json, checks file state, and outputs structured pass/fail report.

dod.criterion: tests/fixture/validate.sh exists, is executable, and runs without errors when invoked with --dry-run
Script accepts a directive ID argument and 'all' to validate all directives
For each directive, script checks: files_modified, files_created, files_deleted, and expected_patterns
Output clearly shows per-directive, per-check pass/fail with expected vs actual values on failure
Running validate.sh on the unmodified fixture correctly reports failures (since no fixes have been applied yet)
